title: Kangaroo Level 1-2 - Practice Set 13: Mixed Review
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: Lina had 4 red balloons and then her friend gave her 3 more. How many balloons does Lina have in total now?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='20' cy='40' r='10'/><circle cx='45' cy='40' r='10'/><circle cx='70' cy='40' r='10'/><circle cx='95' cy='40' r='10'/><text x='115' y='46' font-size='18' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>+</text><circle cx='145' cy='40' r='10'/><circle cx='170' cy='40' r='10'/><circle cx='195' cy='40' r='10'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Four balloons plus three balloons shown as circles.

questions.question_text: Which of the following figures has exactly four straight sides of equal length?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 70' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='30' cy='35' r='20'/><rect x='70' y='15' width='40' height='40'/><polygon points='140,15 165,55 115,55'/><rect x='180' y='20' width='50' height='30'/><ellipse cx='260' cy='35' rx='0' ry='0'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A circle, a square, a triangle, and a rectangle side by side.
questions.solution: A square has 4 equal straight sides.

questions.question_text: Look at the picture. How many small fish are swimming to the right?
questions.image_description: Five fish shapes; three point right and two point left.
questions.solution: Three fish point to the right.

questions.question_text: A pattern goes: heart, star, heart, star, heart, ? What comes next in the pattern?
questions.image_description: Alternating hearts and stars ending with a heart and a question mark.
questions.solution: The pattern alternates heart and star; after a heart comes a star.

questions.question_text: Tom is taller than Ben. Ben is taller than Sam. Who is the shortest of the three?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 240 120' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='30' height='90'/><text x='30' y='115' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>Tom</text><rect x='80' y='45' width='30' height='65'/><text x='90' y='115' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>Ben</text><rect x='140' y='70' width='30' height='40'/><text x='148' y='115' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>Sam</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three bars labeled Tom, Ben, and Sam with decreasing heights.
questions.solution: Since Tom > Ben > Sam, Sam is the shortest.

questions.question_text: Mia has two coins worth 5 cents each and one coin worth 10 cents. How much money does she have?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 70' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='30' cy='35' r='20'/><text x='22' y='40' font-size='12' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>5c</text><circle cx='90' cy='35' r='20'/><text x='82' y='40' font-size='12' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>5c</text><circle cx='160' cy='35' r='24'/><text x='148' y='40' font-size='12' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>10c</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Two 5-cent coins and one 10-cent coin.

questions.question_text: The clock shows the time when Ana starts her breakfast. What time does the clock show?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 120 120' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='60' cy='60' r='50'/><text x='56' y='22' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>12</text><text x='100' y='64' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>3</text><text x='56' y='108' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>6</text><text x='14' y='64' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>9</text><line x1='60' y1='60' x2='60' y2='30'/><line x1='60' y1='60' x2='85' y2='60'/></svg>
questions.image_description: An analog clock with the hour hand on 12 and the minute hand on 3.
questions.solution: Hour hand at 12, minute hand at 3 means 12:15.

questions.question_text: In a box there are only yellow and green marbles. If there are 6 yellow and 2 green, which color has more marbles?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='15' width='240' height='55'/><circle cx='30' cy='45' r='8'/><circle cx='55' cy='45' r='8'/><circle cx='80' cy='45' r='8'/><circle cx='105' cy='45' r='8'/><circle cx='130' cy='45' r='8'/><circle cx='155' cy='45' r='8'/><circle cx='200' cy='45' r='8' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><circle cx='225' cy='45' r='8' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A box with six solid circles (yellow) and two dashed circles (green).
questions.solution: 6 is greater than 2, so yellow has more.

questions.question_text: A basket holds 12 apples. If 5 apples are eaten at lunch, how many apples are still in the basket?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 220 90' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><path d='M20 70 q80 -50 180 0 z'/><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='6'/><circle cx='70' cy='45' r='6'/><circle cx='90' cy='42' r='6'/><circle cx='110' cy='40' r='6'/><circle cx='130' cy='42' r='6'/><circle cx='150' cy='45' r='6'/><circle cx='170' cy='50' r='6'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A basket with apples on top.
questions.solution: 12 - 5 = 7 apples remain.

questions.question_text: A figure is made by joining two identical triangles along one full side. Which shape cannot be formed this way?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 70' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='10,10 50,10 30,50'/><polygon points='30,50 50,10 70,50'/><rect x='100' y='15' width='40' height='40'/><polygon points='170,10 210,10 210,50 170,50'/><circle cx='240' cy='35' r='18'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Examples of shapes that might be built from two triangles alongside a circle.
questions.solution: A circle cannot be made from straight-sided triangles.

questions.question_text: Pablo draws a row of animals: 3 cats, 2 dogs, and 4 rabbits. How many animals did Pablo draw altogether?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='25' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='55' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='85' cy='30' r='10'/><rect x='110' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='140' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><polygon points='180,40 195,15 210,40'/><polygon points='215,40 230,15 245,40'/><polygon points='250,40 265,15 280,40'/><polygon points='180,50 195,45 210,50'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Three circles for cats, two squares for dogs, and four triangles for rabbits.

questions.question_text: The numbers go 2, 4, 6, 8, and then keep adding the same amount. What is the next number in the list?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 50' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><text x='10' y='30' font-size='20' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>2, 4, 6, 8, ?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: The sequence 2, 4, 6, 8, ? written in a row.
questions.solution: The pattern adds 2 each time; 8 + 2 = 10.

questions.question_text: Maya, Noor, and Ravi are standing in a line. Noor is not first. Ravi is last. Who is standing first in the line?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 70' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='100' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='180' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><text x='28' y='46' font-size='14' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>1st</text><text x='107' y='46' font-size='14' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>2nd</text><text x='187' y='46' font-size='14' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>3rd</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three boxes labeled 1st, 2nd, and 3rd places.
questions.solution: Ravi is last and Noor is not first, so Maya is first.

questions.question_text: Four cups hold water: cup P holds 3, cup Q holds 7, cup R holds 5, cup S holds 2. Which cup holds the most water?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 110' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='60' width='30' height='40'/><text x='25' y='108' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>P</text><rect x='80' y='20' width='30' height='80'/><text x='88' y='108' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>Q</text><rect x='140' y='40' width='30' height='60'/><text x='148' y='108' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>R</text><rect x='200' y='75' width='30' height='25'/><text x='208' y='108' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>S</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Four filled cups of different water levels labeled P, Q, R, S.
questions.solution: 7 is the largest of 3, 7, 5, and 2, so cup Q has the most.

questions.question_text: A school bell rings every half hour starting at 9:00. At which of these times does the bell NOT ring?
questions.image_description: A timeline with marks at equal intervals.
questions.solution: Ringing every half hour from 9:00 hits 9:00, 9:30, 10:00, 10:30, but not 9:45.

questions.question_text: A sticker costs 4 cents. Dario buys 3 stickers. How much does he pay in all?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='15' width='40' height='30'/><text x='30' y='35' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>4c</text><rect x='100' y='15' width='40' height='30'/><text x='110' y='35' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>4c</text><rect x='180' y='15' width='40' height='30'/><text x='190' y='35' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>4c</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three stickers each marked 4 cents.

questions.question_text: Four children share 10 cookies. Each child gets the same whole number of cookies, and 2 cookies are left over. How many cookies does each child get?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 300 60' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='45' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='70' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='95' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='120' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='145' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='170' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='195' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='220' cy='30' r='8'/><circle cx='245' cy='30' r='8'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Ten small cookie circles in a row.
questions.solution: 10 - 2 = 8, and 8 divided by 4 children is 2 cookies each.

questions.question_text: A large rectangle is cut along one straight line into two pieces. The two pieces are both rectangles of equal size. Which line was used?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 120' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='100' height='60'/><line x1='70' y1='20' x2='70' y2='80' stroke-dasharray='4 2'/><rect x='150' y='20' width='100' height='60'/><line x1='150' y1='50' x2='250' y2='50' stroke-dasharray='4 2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Two rectangles, one with a vertical dashed line and one with a horizontal dashed line.

options.C: A line through the middle parallel to a side
options.E: A line only in the corner

questions.solution: A straight line parallel to one side through the middle produces two equal rectangles.

questions.question_text: A bookshelf has three rows. The top row has 5 books, the middle row has double that amount, and the bottom row has 3 fewer books than the middle row. How many books are on the bookshelf?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 130' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='10' width='220' height='30'/><rect x='20' y='50' width='220' height='30'/><rect x='20' y='90' width='220' height='30'/><text x='100' y='30' font-size='12' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>top row</text><text x='90' y='70' font-size='12' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>middle row</text><text x='90' y='110' font-size='12' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>bottom row</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A bookshelf diagram with three labeled rows.
questions.solution: Top 5, middle 10, bottom 10 - 3 = 7. Total 5 + 10 + 7 = 22.

questions.question_text: A grid of dots has 4 rows with 3 dots in each row. One extra dot is added below the grid. How many dots are there in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 160 150' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='30' cy='20' r='4'/><circle cx='70' cy='20' r='4'/><circle cx='110' cy='20' r='4'/><circle cx='30' cy='50' r='4'/><circle cx='70' cy='50' r='4'/><circle cx='110' cy='50' r='4'/><circle cx='30' cy='80' r='4'/><circle cx='70' cy='80' r='4'/><circle cx='110' cy='80' r='4'/><circle cx='30' cy='110' r='4'/><circle cx='70' cy='110' r='4'/><circle cx='110' cy='110' r='4'/><circle cx='70' cy='140' r='4'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A 4-by-3 dot grid with one additional dot below.
questions.solution: 4 x 3 = 12 dots in the grid, plus 1 extra equals 13.

questions.question_text: A caterpillar climbs 3 leaves up on sunny days and 1 leaf down on rainy days. It is sunny for 4 days, then rainy for 2 days. How many leaves up is it from where it started?
questions.image_description: A vertical pole with horizontal leaf rungs along it.
questions.solution: Sunny: 4 x 3 = 12 leaves up. Rainy: 2 x 1 = 2 down. 12 - 2 = 10.

questions.question_text: Three friends are holding cards numbered 2, 5, and 8. Kira's number is larger than Leo's. Leo's number is larger than Mia's. Which number is Leo holding?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 90' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='15' width='50' height='60'/><text x='38' y='55' font-size='24' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>2</text><rect x='100' y='15' width='50' height='60'/><text x='118' y='55' font-size='24' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>5</text><rect x='180' y='15' width='50' height='60'/><text x='198' y='55' font-size='24' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>8</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Three number cards showing 2, 5, and 8.
questions.solution: Kira > Leo > Mia, so Leo holds the middle number, which is 5.

questions.question_text: A cartoon starts at 2:00 and lasts 45 minutes. At what time does the cartoon end?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 120' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='60' cy='60' r='45'/><text x='57' y='25' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>12</text><text x='95' y='64' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>3</text><text x='57' y='100' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>6</text><text x='22' y='64' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>9</text><line x1='60' y1='60' x2='88' y2='60'/><line x1='60' y1='60' x2='60' y2='30'/><text x='140' y='65' font-size='14' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>+ 45 minutes</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A clock showing 2:00 and a note adding 45 minutes.
questions.solution: 2:00 plus 45 minutes equals 2:45.

questions.question_text: A pencil costs 6 cents and an eraser costs 9 cents. Elena pays with a 20-cent coin for one of each. How much change does she get back?
questions.image_svg: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 260 80' stroke='currentColor' fill='none' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='60' height='20'/><text x='32' y='35' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>pencil 6c</text><rect x='100' y='20' width='60' height='30'/><text x='108' y='40' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>eraser 9c</text><circle cx='210' cy='35' r='20'/><text x='200' y='40' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>20c</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A pencil, an eraser, and a 20-cent coin.
questions.solution: 6 + 9 = 15 cents cost. Change is 20 - 15 = 5 cents.
